# US Stock Watcher Daily Comprehensive Brief — 2026-07-13

**Moderate / selective risk-on · composite +29 · confidence 44%**

_Data as of: 2026-07-13T16:01:43 · data quality: fresh_

## Executive Summary

The overall market-state score stands at +29 out of 100, placing the current regime in "Moderate / selective risk-on" territory, with a confidence reading of 44% based on four measured components. Six additional inputs — Liquidity, Credit, Earnings, Macro surprises, Valuation, and Positioning — remain unmeasured and have been reweighted out of the composite for this edition.

## Market Diagnosis

**Indices.** Major benchmarks closed lower across the board on the day, with the exception of the Dow. The S&P 500 finished at 7,537.90, down 0.49% on the session, while the Nasdaq-100 lagged more sharply, falling 1.44% to 29,394.54. The Dow Jones Industrial Average held up relatively better, slipping just 0.29% to 52,484.47, and the Russell 2000 declined 0.53% to 2,962.12.

**Market drivers.** Three of the four tracked drivers remain supportive of risk assets: volatility, index trend, and the yield curve are all contributing positively to the composite score. The lone headwind comes from the US dollar, which is working against the risk-on thesis.

**Breadth & concentration.** Consistent with the executive summary, the overall market-state score of +29/100 continues to characterize the tape as Moderate / selective risk-on, with confidence held at 44% on four measured components. The same six factors — Liquidity, Credit, Earnings, Macro surprises, Valuation, and Positioning — remain excluded from the calculation pending further data.

**Sector & rotation.** Leadership today is concentrated in Financials, Industrials, and Health Care, while Real Estate, Consumer Staples, and Energy are lagging the broader tape — a rotation pattern that points to cyclical, growth-sensitive areas outperforming defensives even as headline indices retreat.

**Rates, dollar & commodities.** The 10-year Treasury yield sits at 4.54%, while the US Dollar Index (DXY) is modestly firmer at 101.14, up 0.17% on the day. Commodities showed a pronounced divergence: WTI crude oil surged 4.68% to $74.75, while gold fell back 2.06% to $4,019.50.

## Outlook

**Short term.** The near-term bias remains constructive, anchored to the "Moderate / selective risk-on" market state and its 44% confidence level. This view would flip should the overall composite score cross back below zero.

**Medium term.** The medium-term stance is similarly constructive, reflecting the same "Moderate / selective risk-on" classification at 44% confidence. As with the short-term view, a move of the overall score below zero would invalidate this bias.

**Medium-to-long term.** Looking further out, the constructive bias persists on the same basis — a "Moderate / selective risk-on" reading with 44% confidence — with the zero-line on the composite score serving as the key trigger for a reassessment.

## Top News

Markets were dominated today by escalating geopolitical tension in the Middle East. US Treasury yields rose following reported attacks between the United States and Iran, according to The Edge Malaysia, while Crypto Briefing reported that a US naval blockade is impacting shipping through the Strait of Hormuz amid the rising yield backdrop. Yahoo Finance captured the market reaction directly: the Dow rose while the S&P 500 and Nasdaq slipped as the US and Iran exchanged fire and oil prices jumped — consistent with the day's index moves and the 4.68% pop in WTI crude noted above. Elsewhere, Stock Titan reported that Toronto-Dominion Bank (NYSE: TD) priced capped buffered notes linked to the S&P 500. The Globe and Mail published a comparative look at the PBJ and FTXG food & beverage ETFs against the S&P 500. Separately, the WSJ's live markets coverage highlighted AI-related jitters weighing on the Nasdaq, with SK Hynix shares slumping as part of that theme.
